info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: During a microscopic examination of a stained stool specimen from an asymptomatic patient, a laboratory technologist identifies a large spherical cyst (20 µm) containing eight distinct nuclei and an eccentric karyosome. Which of the following has been identified?

q2.prompt: A 40-year-old fisherman from a coastal Southeast Asian village presents with chronic profuse watery diarrhea, severe weight loss, hypoalbuminemia, and muscle wasting, with gurgling stomach. He reports frequent consumption of raw freshwater fish. Examination shows muscle wasting and signs of malnutrition. Which of the following is the key characteristic of the egg of the causative agent?

options.label: Pitted shell with bipolar flattened plugs

q3.prompt: A 48-year-old man with a history of corticosteroid therapy for asthma develops refractory septic shock caused by *Escherichia coli* bacteremia. Bronchoscopy reveals numerous rhabditiform larvae in the endotracheal secretions. Which mechanism explains how the bacteria reached the bloodstream?

options.label: Direct penetration of the larvae through the skin
Ingestion of infectious eggs contaminating food
Migration of adult worms into the biliary tract
Larval transport of bacteria during internal autoinfection
Entry of larvae through the cribriform plate

q4.prompt: A patient is treated for an intestinal fluke infection with Praziquantel. Which of the following best describes the primary mechanism of action of this medication against the parasite?

options.label: Promotion of calcium influx leading to spastic paralysis
Inhibition of parasite haemoglobin digestion in the vacuole
Prevention of DNA synthesis by inhibiting reverse transcriptase
Inhibition of microtubule polymerization by binding to β-tubulin
Activation of chloride channels causing flaccid paralysis

q5.prompt: During a laboratory investigation of a stool specimen, a white, ribbon-like gravid proglottid is recovered. India ink injection reveals 22 primary lateral uterine branches on one side of the segment. Which parasite is identified?

q6.prompt: A 35-year-old traveler returning from Thailand presents with a three-day history of bloody, mucoid diarrhea and tenesmus; stool microscopy shows motile trophozoites containing ingested red blood cells. Which finding is most likely to be observed during a colonoscopy with biopsy?

options.label: Multiple inflammatory nodules in the lamina propria
Diffuse inflammatory foci throughout the mucosa
Squamous cell carcinoma of the intestinal wall
Presence of adult worms embedded in the mucosal layer

q7.prompt: A 35-year-old male with a history of AIDS and a CD4+ T-lymphocyte count of 80 cells/mm^3 presents with a 3-day history of worsening headache and left upper limb weakness. Physical examination reveals oral thrush and significant weight loss. A brain MRI demonstrates multiple ring-enhancing lesions. The patient reports that he frequently consumes raw, unwashed vegetables harvested from a garden where several stray cats are known to roam. Through which parasitic stage did this patient most likely acquire the primary infection?

q8.prompt: A 10-year-old boy is brought to the emergency department with a 2-day history of explosive headache, high fever, and neck stiffness. His parents report that he was diving into a local warm freshwater pond six days ago. On physical examination, he is lethargic and demonstrates signs of meningeal irritation. A lumbar puncture is performed, and the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) appears grayish and hemorrhagic with a significantly increased opening pressure. Which of the following CSF profiles is most consistent with the expected findings for this patient's diagnosis?

options.label: Normal glucose, normal protein, lymphocyte predominance
Normal glucose, high protein, neutrophil predominance
Low glucose, high protein, lymphocyte predominance
Normal glucose, high protein, lymphocyte predominance
Low glucose, high protein, neutrophil predominance

q9.prompt: A 45-year-old male from Northeastern Thailand presents with intermittent upper abdominal pain and jaundice. He reports a lifelong dietary habit of consuming "koi pla," a dish containing raw freshwater fish. A stool examination reveals yellow-brown, flask-shaped eggs with a distinct shoulder and a terminal knob. What is the infective stage of the parasite responsible for this patient's condition?

q10.prompt: A routine stool examination of an asymptomatic child reveals brownish, barrel-shaped eggs with characteristic clear mucoid plugs at both ends. Which parasite does this represent?
